steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that longitudinal studies like NILS are expensive because they span many years. Public opinion studies don't need that multi-year approach — so figure out what follows for their cost.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'considerably less expensive': If public opinion studies don't require the time-intensive longitudinal methods that drive NILS's cost, they should cost much less. ✓
  • Choice B — 'more expensive than NILS': This reverses the cost direction — without the multi-year burden, cost goes down, not up. ✗
  • Choice C — 'equally expensive': The passage draws a direct contrast; skipping the multi-year method removes the cost driver. ✗
  • Choice D — 'more reliable': The passage never compares reliability between the two study types. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
